content: The group reagent $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{Cl}(\mathrm{s})$ and aqueous $\mathrm{NH}_3$ will precipitate which of the following ion?
explanation: $$ \text { Nylon in presence of } \mathrm{NaCl} \text { is used for detection of group III cations which are } \mathrm{Al}^{3+}, \mathrm{Fe}^{3+}, \mathrm{Cr}^{3+} $$

content: The sodium fusion extract is boiled with concentrated nitric acid while testing for halogens. By doing so, it
explanation: $$ \text { It is done so that if there is any } \mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{~s} \text { or } \mathrm{NaCN} \text {, it can be decomposed. } $$

content: In the analysis of III group basic radicals of salts, the purpose of adding $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{Cl}(s)$ to $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{OH}$ is

options.content: to precipitate the radicals of group IV and V

explanation: In the analysis of III group basic radicals of salts, the purpose of adding $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{Cl}(s)$ to $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{OH}$ is, to suppress the dissociation of $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{OH}$ due to the common ion effect.
